title: find_chunk_parallels data payload
description: v0.19.0 sub-tablet chunk-parallel detector. Surfaces every contiguous matched-trigram run ≥ minChunkLen between a source tablet and any candidate tablet in the corpus as a primary object (chunk_start + chunk_length + host_tablets[]). Reuses the fuzzy 2-of-3 inverted-index infrastructure from fuzzyParallels.ts; the alignment walk is identical to find_embedded_fragments' but emits every maximal run instead of the longest. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

chunk_key.description: Canonical "<start>:<length>" identifier within the source tablet.

chunk_start.description: Position in the source's ordered-trigram stream where the matched run begins.

chunk_length.description: Length of the run in trigram positions (sign count ≈ chunk_length + 2 for clean tablets).

chunk_signs.description: Reconstructed sign sequence. When trigrams_ordered skips X-heavy positions, discontinuities are marked with ' … '.

cross_genre_count.description: Hosts whose primary genre differs from the source's primary genre. Zero when either side lacks fragment-metadata.

cross_period_count.description: Hosts whose period differs from the source's period. Zero when either side lacks fragment-metadata.

host_genres_spanned.description: Count of DISTINCT host primary genres for this chunk, independent of source-metadata availability. v0.19.1 primitive for downstream cross-curricular ranking; differs from cross_genre_count, which is anchored to the source's genre.

novelty_score.description: (1 / log2(2 + host_count)) × (1 + 0.5 × cross_genre_fraction + 0.5 × cross_period_fraction). High for rare chunks crossing curriculum boundaries; low for ubiquitous colophon-formulae.

source_coverage_pct.description: Percentage of source trigram positions covered by ≥1 returned chunk.

distinct_chunks.description: Total chunks surviving filters before topK truncation.
